matches.match_rationale: Vendor lock-in is perpetuated when data is trapped in proprietary schemas — migrating away requires costly manual schema mapping. AI-assisted ETL mapping can automatically infer correspondences between the incumbent vendor's export format and a target schema, dramatically reducing the switching cost that keeps operators trapped. For multi-site operators with years of accumulated data in siloed formats, this is the concrete technical blocker that prevents exit.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A tool that ingests a vendor data export (CSV, XML, JSON), maps it to a neutral canonical schema, and generates transformation scripts — reducing a 6-month migration project to weeks. Positioned as a 'vendor exit accelerator' for education operators.

matches.match_rationale: One of the primary switching costs is rewriting integrations — the operator's existing systems are wired to the incumbent vendor's proprietary APIs. AI code generation can automatically produce adapter layers, API wrappers, and integration connectors that replicate the incumbent's interface over a new system, neutralising integration rewrite costs. This directly attacks the 'rewriting all integrations' mechanism that makes switching prohibitive.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI coding assistant workflow that reads existing integration code, reverse-engineers the vendor API surface, and generates equivalent adapters targeting a new vendor or an open standard — turning a months-long dev project into a days-long generation task.

matches.match_rationale: Operators trapped by vendor lock-in often compensate by manually bridging gaps — exporting data, reformatting it, re-importing into adjacent systems, and maintaining workarounds when the vendor won't build requested features. A task automation agent can orchestrate these multi-step bridge workflows across systems, reducing the day-to-day pain of operating a constrained ecosystem while an exit strategy matures. It also generates documented evidence of exactly which workflows the operator would need to replicate in a new system — directly informing the exit plan.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An autonomous workflow agent that monitors vendor system events, extracts data via whatever export mechanism is available, and pushes it into other tools (finance, HRIS, reporting) — acting as a vendor-agnostic integration layer the operator controls.

matches.match_rationale: Before committing to an exit strategy, education operators need a clear-eyed assessment of alternative vendors, total switching costs, and contractual risk — analysis that currently requires expensive consultants or months of internal effort. A research agent can autonomously gather vendor comparison data, scrape contract terms databases, synthesise peer operator experiences, and produce a structured vendor risk and exit feasibility report specific to multi-site education contexts.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A research agent that accepts a vendor name and contract terms as input and produces a structured briefing: comparable vendors, estimated migration costs, contractual exit clauses, and a prioritised recommendation — giving leadership the evidence to act rather than stay frozen.
